1→"""Transcript discovery — file scanning and project matching."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import re
     6→from pathlib import Path
     7→
     8→from _transcript_common import CLIENTS, slugify
     9→from _transcript_parse import ParsedTranscript
    10→
    11→
    12→def default_global_transcript_root() -> Path:
    13→    return Path.home() / ".memorytree" / "transcripts"
    14→
    15→
    16→def default_client_roots() -> dict[str, Path]:
    17→    home = Path.home()
    18→    return {
    19→        "codex": home / ".codex",
    20→        "claude": home / ".claude",
    21→        "gemini": home / ".gemini",
    22→    }
    23→
    24→
    25→def discover_source_files(clients: set[str] | None = None) -> list[tuple[str, Path]]:
    26→    requested = clients or CLIENTS
    27→    roots = default_client_roots()
    28→    patterns = {
    29→        "codex": ["sessions/**/*.jsonl"],
    30→        "claude": ["projects/**/*.jsonl"],
    31→        "gemini": [
    32→            "tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.json",
    33→            "tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.jsonl",
    34→            "history/**/*.json",
    35→            "history/**/*.jsonl",
    36→            "chats/**/*.json",
    37→            "chats/**/*.jsonl",
    38→        ],
    39→    }
    40→    matches: list[tuple[str, Path]] = []
    41→    seen: set[str] = set()
    42→    for client in sorted(requested):
    43→        root = roots.get(client)
    44→        if root is None or not root.exists():
    45→            continue
    46→        for pattern in patterns[client]:
    47→            for path in root.glob(pattern):
    48→                if not path.is_file():
    49→                    continue
    50→                key = path.resolve().as_posix().lower()
    51→                if key in seen:
    52→                    continue
    53→                seen.add(key)
    54→                matches.append((client, path.resolve()))
    55→    matches.sort(
    56→        key=lambda item: (safe_file_mtime(item[1]), item[1].as_posix().lower()),
    57→        reverse=True,
    58→    )
    59→    return matches
    60→
    61→
    62→def infer_project_slug(parsed: ParsedTranscript) -> str:
    63→    if parsed.cwd:
    64→        try:
    65→            cwd_path = Path(parsed.cwd)
    66→            return slugify(cwd_path.name, fallback="project")
    67→        except OSError:
    68→            pass
    69→    if parsed.client == "claude":
    70→        parent_name = parsed.source_path.parent.name
    71→        parent_name = re.sub(r"^[a-z]--", "", parent_name.lower())
    72→        return slugify(parent_name, fallback="project")
    73→    if parsed.client == "gemini" and parsed.source_path.parent.name:
    74→        return slugify(parsed.source_path.parent.name, fallback="project")
    75→    return "unknown-project"
    76→
    77→
    78→def transcript_matches_repo(parsed: ParsedTranscript, repo_root: Path, repo_slug: str) -> bool:
    79→    repo_root = repo_root.resolve()
    80→    if parsed.cwd:
    81→        try:
    82→            cwd_path = Path(parsed.cwd).resolve()
    83→            if cwd_path == repo_root:
    84→                return True
    85→            if repo_root in cwd_path.parents:
    86→                return True
    87→        except OSError:
    88→            pass
    89→    return project_slugs_match(infer_project_slug(parsed), repo_slug)
    90→
    91→
    92→def project_slugs_match(left: str, right: str) -> bool:
    93→    return bool(left and right and left == right and left != "unknown-project")
    94→
    95→
    96→def safe_file_mtime(path: Path) -> float:
    97→    try:
    98→        return path.stat().st_mtime
    99→    except OSError:
   100→        return 0.0
   101→

1→"""Transcript import — orchestration, cleaning, and manifest management."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import json
     6→import shutil
     7→from datetime import datetime, timezone
     8→from pathlib import Path
     9→from typing import Any
    10→
    11→from _transcript_common import (
    12→    load_json,
    13→    normalize_timestamp,
    14→    sha256_file,
    15→    slugify,
    16→    timestamp_partition,
    17→    yaml_escape,
    18→)
    19→from _transcript_index import upsert_search_index
    20→from _transcript_parse import ParsedTranscript
    21→
    22→
    23→def import_transcript(
    24→    parsed: ParsedTranscript,
    25→    root: Path,
    26→    global_root: Path,
    27→    project_slug: str,
    28→    raw_upload_permission: str,
    29→    mirror_to_repo: bool = True,
    30→) -> dict[str, Any]:
    31→    imported_at = normalize_timestamp(datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    32→    raw_sha256 = sha256_file(parsed.source_path)
    33→    session_label = slugify(parsed.session_id, fallback=slugify(parsed.source_path.stem, fallback="session"))
    34→    artifact_stem = f"{session_label}__{raw_sha256[:8]}"
    35→    source_suffix = parsed.source_path.suffix or ".txt"
    36→
    37→    year_token, month_token = timestamp_partition(parsed.started_at or imported_at)
    38→
    39→    repo_root = root / "Memory" / "06_transcripts"
    40→    repo_raw_path = repo_root / "raw" / parsed.client / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}{source_suffix}"
    41→    repo_clean_path = repo_root / "clean" / parsed.client / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}.md"
    42→    repo_manifest_path = repo_root / "manifests" / parsed.client / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}.json"
    43→
    44→    global_raw_path = global_root / "raw" / parsed.client / project_slug / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}{source_suffix}"
    45→    global_clean_path = global_root / "clean" / parsed.client / project_slug / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}.md"
    46→    global_manifest_path = global_root / "index" / "manifests" / parsed.client / project_slug / year_token / month_token / f"{artifact_stem}.json"
    47→    global_event_log_path = global_root / "index" / "sessions.jsonl"
    48→    global_db_path = global_root / "index" / "search.sqlite"
    49→
    50→    for path in (
    51→        global_raw_path,
    52→        global_clean_path,
    53→        global_manifest_path,
    54→        global_event_log_path,
    55→        global_db_path,
    56→    ):
    57→        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    58→    if mirror_to_repo:
    59→        for path in (repo_raw_path, repo_clean_path, repo_manifest_path):
    60→            path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    61→
    62→    if mirror_to_repo:
    63→        copy_file(parsed.source_path, repo_raw_path)
    64→    copy_file(parsed.source_path, global_raw_path)
    65→
    66→    manifest = {
    67→        "client": parsed.client,
    68→        "project": project_slug,
    69→        "session_id": parsed.session_id,
    70→        "title": parsed.title,
    71→        "started_at": parsed.started_at,
    72→        "imported_at": imported_at,
    73→        "cwd": parsed.cwd,
    74→        "branch": parsed.branch,
    75→        "raw_source_path": parsed.source_path.as_posix(),
    76→        "raw_sha256": raw_sha256,
    77→        "raw_upload_permission": raw_upload_permission,
    78→        "repo_raw_path": repo_raw_path.relative_to(root).as_posix() if mirror_to_repo else "",
    79→        "repo_clean_path": repo_clean_path.relative_to(root).as_posix() if mirror_to_repo else "",
    80→        "repo_manifest_path": repo_manifest_path.relative_to(root).as_posix() if mirror_to_repo else "",
    81→        "global_raw_path": global_raw_path.as_posix(),
    82→        "global_clean_path": global_clean_path.as_posix(),
    83→        "global_manifest_path": global_manifest_path.as_posix(),
    84→        "message_count": len(parsed.messages),
    85→        "tool_event_count": len(parsed.tool_events),
    86→        "cleaning_mode": "deterministic-code",
    87→        "repo_mirror_enabled": mirror_to_repo,
    88→    }
    89→    existing_global_manifest = load_json(global_manifest_path)
    90→    manifest = preserve_existing_import_timestamp(existing_global_manifest, manifest)
    91→
    92→    if mirror_to_repo:
    93→        write_clean_markdown(parsed, manifest, repo_clean_path)
    94→    write_clean_markdown(parsed, manifest, global_clean_path)
    95→    if mirror_to_repo:
    96→        write_json(repo_manifest_path, manifest)
    97→    append_to_event_log = existing_global_manifest is None or existing_global_manifest != manifest
    98→    write_json(global_manifest_path, manifest)
    99→    if append_to_event_log:
   100→        append_jsonl(global_event_log_path, manifest)
   101→    upsert_search_index(global_db_path, manifest)
   102→
   103→    result = dict(manifest)
   104→    result["global_index_db"] = global_db_path.as_posix()
   105→    return result
   106→
   107→
   108→def transcript_has_content(parsed: ParsedTranscript) -> bool:
   109→    return bool(parsed.messages or parsed.tool_events)
   110→
   111→
   112→def write_clean_markdown(parsed: ParsedTranscript, manifest: dict[str, Any], path: Path) -> None:
   113→    lines = [
   114→        "---",
   115→        f"client: {manifest['client']}",
   116→        f"project: {manifest['project']}",
   117→        f"session_id: {manifest['session_id']}",
   118→        f"title: {yaml_escape(manifest['title'])}",
   119→        f"started_at: {manifest['started_at']}",
   120→        f"imported_at: {manifest['imported_at']}",
   121→        f"cwd: {yaml_escape(manifest['cwd'])}",
   122→        f"branch: {yaml_escape(manifest['branch'])}",
   123→        f"raw_source_path: {yaml_escape(manifest['raw_source_path'])}",
   124→        f"raw_sha256: {manifest['raw_sha256']}",
   125→        f"raw_upload_permission: {manifest['raw_upload_permission']}",
   126→        f"cleaning_mode: {manifest['cleaning_mode']}",
   127→        "---",
   128→        "",
   129→        f"# {manifest['title'] or manifest['session_id']}",
   130→        "",
   131→        "## Metadata",
   132→        f"- Client: `{manifest['client']}`",
   133→        f"- Project: `{manifest['project']}`",
   134→        f"- Session ID: `{manifest['session_id']}`",
   135→        f"- Started At: `{manifest['started_at']}`",
   136→        f"- Imported At: `{manifest['imported_at']}`",
   137→        f"- Raw SHA256: `{manifest['raw_sha256']}`",
   138→        f"- Raw Source: `{manifest['raw_source_path']}`",
   139→        f"- Repo Raw Path: `{manifest['repo_raw_path']}`",
   140→        f"- Repo Clean Path: `{manifest['repo_clean_path']}`",
   141→        "",
   142→        "## Messages",
   143→    ]
   144→
   145→    if parsed.messages:
   146→        for index, message in enumerate(parsed.messages, start=1):
   147→            lines.extend(
   148→                [
   149→                    f"### {index}. {message.role}",
   150→                    f"- Timestamp: `{message.timestamp or manifest['started_at']}`",
   151→                    "",
   152→                    message.text,
   153→                    "",
   154→                ]
   155→            )
   156→    else:
   157→        lines.extend(
   158→            [
   159→                "No user or assistant messages were extracted deterministically from the source transcript.",
   160→                "",
   161→            ]
   162→        )
   163→
   164→    lines.append("## Tool Events")
   165→    if parsed.tool_events:
   166→        for event in parsed.tool_events:
   167→            lines.append(f"- `{event.timestamp or manifest['started_at']}` {event.summary}")
   168→    else:
   169→        lines.append("- No tool events were extracted.")
   170→    lines.append("")
   171→
   172→    path.write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")
   173→
   174→
   175→def preserve_existing_import_timestamp(existing: dict[str, Any] | None, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
   176→    if existing is None:
   177→        return payload
   178→    if manifest_signature(existing) != manifest_signature(payload):
   179→        return payload
   180→    preserved = dict(payload)
   181→    imported_at = existing.get("imported_at")
   182→    if isinstance(imported_at, str) and imported_at:
   183→        preserved["imported_at"] = imported_at
   184→    return preserved
   185→
   186→
   187→def manifest_signature(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
   188→    return {key: value for key, value in payload.items() if key != "imported_at"}
   189→
   190→
   191→def manifest_changed(path: Path, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   192→    if not path.exists():
   193→        return True
   194→    try:
   195→        current = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   196→    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
   197→        return True
   198→    return current != payload
   199→
   200→
   201→def write_json(path: Path, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   202→    path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
   203→
   204→
   205→def append_jsonl(path: Path, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   206→    with path.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
   207→        handle.write(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
   208→
   209→
   210→def copy_file(source: Path, destination: Path) -> None:
   211→    if source.resolve() == destination.resolve():
   212→        return
   213→    shutil.copy2(source, destination)
   214→

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→Upgrade a repository to MemoryTree without overwriting existing repo policy files.
     4→"""
     5→
     6→from __future__ import annotations
     7→
     8→import argparse
     9→import json
    10→import re
    11→from pathlib import Path
    12→
    13→from _locale_utils import normalize_locale
    14→from _scaffold_utils import (
    15→    build_datetime,
    16→    create_memory_dirs,
    17→    find_external_policy_sources,
    18→    find_latest_chat_log_file,
    19→    find_latest_goal_file,
    20→    find_latest_todo_file,
    21→    resolve_scaffold_paths,
    22→    resolve_template_dir,
    23→    scaffold_content_files,
    24→    write_template,
    25→)
    26→
    27→
    28→def main() -> int:
    29→    args = parse_args()
    30→    root = Path(args.root).resolve()
    31→    if not root.exists():
    32→        raise SystemExit(f"root does not exist: {root}")
    33→
    34→    skill_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
    35→    templates = resolve_template_dir(skill_root, root, args.locale)
    36→    effective_locale = normalize_locale(args.locale, root)
    37→    initial_state = detect_state(root)
    38→
    39→    dt = build_datetime(args.date, args.time)
    40→
    41→    created_dirs = create_memory_dirs(root)
    42→    paths = resolve_scaffold_paths(root, dt)
    43→    created_files, preserved_files = scaffold_content_files(
    44→        paths, templates, args.goal_summary, args.project_name, force=False,
    45→    )
    46→
    47→    agents_path = root / "AGENTS.md"
    48→    agents_action = "preserved_existing"
    49→    if not agents_path.exists():
    50→        if write_template(templates / "agents.md", agents_path, False, {}):
    51→            created_files.append("AGENTS.md")
    52→            agents_action = "created"
    53→    else:
    54→        preserved_files.append("AGENTS.md")
    55→    merge_required = agents_action == "preserved_existing" and needs_agents_merge(agents_path)
    56→
    57→    result = {
    58→        "state_before": initial_state,
    59→        "state_after": detect_state(root),
    60→        "requested_locale": args.locale,
    61→        "effective_locale": effective_locale,
    62→        "created_dirs": created_dirs,
    63→        "created_files": created_files,
    64→        "preserved_files": preserved_files,
    65→        "agents_action": agents_action,
    66→        "agents_merge_required": merge_required,
    67→    }
    68→
    69→    if args.format == "json":
    70→        print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False))
    71→    else:
    72→        print(format_result_text(result))
    73→    return 0
    74→
    75→
    76→def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
    77→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Upgrade a repository to MemoryTree safely.")
    78→    parser.add_argument("--root", default=".", help="Target repository root.")
    79→    parser.add_argument("--project-name", default="this project", help="Project name used in generated chat logs.")
    80→    parser.add_argument(
    81→        "--goal-summary",
    82→        default="Describe the long-term project goal here.",
    83→        help="Fallback north-star summary when a goal file must be created.",
    84→    )
    85→    parser.add_argument("--locale", default="auto", help="Requested locale: auto, en, or zh-cn.")
    86→    parser.add_argument("--date", default="", help="Override date as YYYY-MM-DD for deterministic runs.")
    87→    parser.add_argument(
    88→        "--time",
    89→        default="",
    90→        help="Override time as HH:MM. If --date is omitted, use the current local date.",
    91→    )
    92→    parser.add_argument("--format", choices=("text", "json"), default="text", help="Output format.")
    93→    return parser.parse_args()
    94→
    95→
    96→def detect_state(root: Path) -> str:
    97→    memory_dir = root / "Memory"
    98→    goal_dir = root / "Memory" / "01_goals"
    99→    todo_dir = root / "Memory" / "02_todos"
   100→    chat_dir = root / "Memory" / "03_chat_logs"
   101→    agents_path = root / "AGENTS.md"
   102→    policy_sources = find_external_policy_sources(root)
   103→
   104→    latest_goal = find_latest_goal_file(goal_dir)
   105→    goal_version = extract_goal_version(latest_goal)
   106→    has_goal = latest_goal is not None
   107→    has_todo = goal_version is not None and find_latest_todo_file(todo_dir, goal_version) is not None
   108→    has_chat = find_latest_chat_log_file(chat_dir) is not None
   109→    has_agents = agents_path.is_file()
   110→    agents_ready = has_agents and not needs_agents_merge(agents_path)
   111→
   112→    if not memory_dir.exists():
   113→        return "partial" if has_agents or policy_sources else "not-installed"
   114→    if has_goal and has_todo and has_chat and agents_ready:
   115→        return "installed"
   116→    return "partial"
   117→
   118→
   119→def extract_goal_version(path: Path | None) -> str | None:
   120→    if path is None:
   121→        return None
   122→    match = re.fullmatch(r"goal_v(\d{3})_\d{8}\.md", path.name)
   123→    if match is None:
   124→        return None
   125→    return match.group(1)
   126→
   127→
   128→def needs_agents_merge(path: Path) -> bool:
   129→    try:
   130→        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore").lower()
   131→    except OSError:
   132→        return True
   133→
   134→    required_checks = [
   135→        has_read_order_signal(text),
   136→        has_memory_layout_signal(text),
   137→        has_active_todo_rule(text),
   138→        has_append_only_chat_log_rule(text),
   139→        has_repo_safety_rule(text),
   140→        has_commit_title_rule(text),
   141→        has_dedicated_memorytree_pr_flow(text),
   142→        has_memorytree_only_scope_rule(text),
   143→        has_auto_merge_rule(text),
   144→    ]
   145→    return not all(required_checks)
   146→
   147→
   148→def matches_any(text: str, patterns: tuple[str, ...]) -> bool:
   149→    return any(re.search(pattern, text) for pattern in patterns)
   150→
   151→
   152→def matches_all_concepts(text: str, concept_groups: tuple[tuple[str, ...], ...]) -> bool:
   153→    return all(matches_any(text, patterns) for patterns in concept_groups)
   154→
   155→
   156→def has_read_order_signal(text: str) -> bool:
   157→    return matches_any(
   158→        text,
   159→        (
   160→            r"\bread order\b",
   161→            r"\bread .* in order\b",
   162→            r"\u8bfb\u53d6\u987a\u5e8f",
   163→            r"\u6309\u6b64\u987a\u5e8f\u8bfb\u53d6",
   164→        ),
   165→    )
   166→
   167→
   168→def has_memory_layout_signal(text: str) -> bool:
   169→    required_paths = (
   170→        (r"memory/01_goals", r"\b01_goals\b"),
   171→        (r"memory/02_todos", r"\b02_todos\b"),
   172→        (r"memory/03_chat_logs", r"\b03_chat_logs\b"),
   173→    )
   174→    return matches_all_concepts(text, required_paths)
   175→
   176→
   177→def has_active_todo_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   178→    return matches_any(
   179→        text,
   180→        (
   181→            r"keep .*todo .*synchron",
   182→            r"todo .*aligned .*active goal",
   183→            r"(sync|align|keep|maintain|bind).{0,50}(active|current).{0,20}todo.{0,50}(active|current).{0,20}goal",
   184→            r"(active|current).{0,20}todo.{0,50}(sync|align|match|bound).{0,50}(active|current).{0,20}goal",
   185→            r"\u5f53\u524d\u5f85\u529e\u5fc5\u987b\u4e0e\u5f53\u524d\u76ee\u6807\u4fdd\u6301\u540c\u6b65",
   186→            r"\u5f85\u529e\u5fc5\u987b\u4e0e\u5f53\u524d\u76ee\u6807\u4fdd\u6301\u540c\u6b65",
   187→        ),
   188→    )
   189→
   190→
   191→def has_append_only_chat_log_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   192→    return matches_any(
   193→        text,
   194→        (
   195→            r"append[- ]only .*chat log",
   196→            r"append[- ]only records",
   197→            r"treat chat logs as append[- ]only",
   198→            r"never rewrite or delete prior entries",
   199→            r"\u5bf9\u8bdd\u65e5\u5fd7\u53ea\u8ffd\u52a0",
   200→            r"\u7981\u6b62\u91cd\u5199\u6216\u5220\u9664\u65e2\u6709\u5185\u5bb9",
   201→        ),
   202→    )
   203→
   204→
   205→def has_repo_safety_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   206→    concept_groups = (
   207→        (r"\bpr\b", r"\bpull request\b", r"\bmerge request\b", r"\u62c9\u53d6\u8bf7\u6c42", r"\u5408\u5e76\u8bf7\u6c42"),
   208→        (r"\bci\b", r"\bchecks?\b", r"\bpipeline\b", r"\be2e\b", r"\u68c0\u67e5", r"\u6d41\u6c34\u7ebf"),
   209→        (r"\breview(?:er|ers)?\b", r"\bapproval(?:s)?\b", r"\u8bc4\u5ba1", r"\u5ba1\u6279"),
   210→        (r"\bbranch(?:es)?\b", r"\bprotected branch(?:es)?\b", r"\u5206\u652f"),
   211→    )
   212→    return matches_all_concepts(text, concept_groups) and matches_any(
   213→        text,
   214→        (
   215→            r"\brepository\b",
   216→            r"\brepo\b",
   217→            r"\bproject\b",
   218→            r"\bhost\b",
   219→            r"\brule(?:s)?\b",
   220→            r"\brequirement(?:s)?\b",
   221→            r"\bpolicy\b",
   222→            r"\bcontrol(?:s)?\b",
   223→            r"\u4ed3\u5e93",
   224→            r"\u89c4\u5219",
   225→            r"\u8981\u6c42",
   226→        ),
   227→    )
   228→
   229→
   230→def has_commit_title_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   231→    return matches_any(
   232→        text,
   233→        (
   234→            r"\bmemorytree\([^)]*\):",
   235→            r"\b[a-z]+\((memorytree)\):",
   236→            r"\bmemorytree:",
   237→            r"memorytree-scoped commit title",
   238→            r"memorytree-specific commit title",
   239→            r"use .*memorytree.*commit title",
   240→            r"\u63d0\u4ea4\u6807\u9898.*memorytree",
   241→        ),
   242→    )
   243→
   244→
   245→def has_dedicated_memorytree_pr_flow(text: str) -> bool:
   246→    concept_groups = (
   247→        (r"\bmemorytree\b", r"\u8bb0\u5fc6"),
   248→        (r"\bbranch(?:es)?\b", r"\u5206\u652f"),
   249→        (r"\bpr\b", r"\bpull request\b", r"\bmerge request\b", r"\u62c9\u53d6\u8bf7\u6c42", r"\u5408\u5e76\u8bf7\u6c42"),
   250→        (r"\bdedicated\b", r"\bseparate\b", r"\bisolated\b", r"\bown\b", r"\u4e13\u7528", r"\u72ec\u7acb"),
   251→    )
   252→    return matches_all_concepts(text, concept_groups)
   253→
   254→
   255→def has_memorytree_only_scope_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   256→    return matches_any(
   257→        text,
   258→        (
   259→            r"memorytree[- ]owned changes",
   260→            r"memorytree[- ]owned files",
   261→            r"memorytree-managed files",
   262→            r"(stage|push|commit).{0,40}only.{0,40}memorytree[- ]owned.{0,20}(files|changes)?",
   263→            r"(stage|push|commit).{0,40}only.{0,40}memorytree.{0,20}(files|changes|diff)",
   264→            r"only.{0,40}memorytree.{0,20}(files|changes|diff)",
   265→            r"\u4ec5\u81ea\u52a8\u63d0\u4ea4\u548c\u63a8\u9001.*memorytree.*\u53d8\u66f4",
   266→            r"\u7531 memorytree \u7ba1\u7406",
   267→        ),
   268→    )
   269→
   270→
   271→def has_auto_merge_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   272→    return matches_any(
   273→        text,
   274→        (
   275→            r"auto-merge only when .*permit",
   276→            r"only when repository rules permit",
   277→            r"only when repo rules permit",
   278→            r"only enable auto-merge when.{0,60}(required )?(approvals|checks|reviews?)",
   279→            r"auto-merge.{0,60}(required approvals|required checks|repository rules)",
   280→            r"\u4ec5\u5728\u4ed3\u5e93\u89c4\u5219\u5141\u8bb8\u65f6\u624d\u5f00\u542f\u81ea\u52a8\u5408\u5e76",
   281→        ),
   282→    )
   283→
   284→
   285→def format_result_text(result: dict) -> str:
   286→    lines = [
   287→        f"state_before: {result['state_before']}",
   288→        f"state_after: {result['state_after']}",
   289→        f"effective_locale: {result['effective_locale']}",
   290→        f"agents_action: {result['agents_action']}",
   291→        f"agents_merge_required: {str(result['agents_merge_required']).lower()}",
   292→    ]
   293→    if result["created_dirs"]:
   294→        lines.append("created_dirs:")
   295→        lines.extend(f"- {item}" for item in result["created_dirs"])
   296→    if result["created_files"]:
   297→        lines.append("created_files:")
   298→        lines.extend(f"- {item}" for item in result["created_files"])
   299→    if result["preserved_files"]:
   300→        lines.append("preserved_files:")
   301→        lines.extend(f"- {item}" for item in result["preserved_files"])
   302→    return "\n".join(lines)
   303→
   304→
   305→if __name__ == "__main__":
   306→    raise SystemExit(main())
   307→

    92→def build_datetime(date_value: str, time_value: str) -> datetime:
    93→    if date_value and time_value:
    94→        return datetime.strptime(f"{date_value} {time_value}", "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")
    95→    if date_value:
    96→        return datetime.strptime(f"{date_value} 00:00", "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")
    97→    if time_value:
    98→        today = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    99→        return datetime.strptime(f"{today} {time_value}", "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")
   100→    return datetime.now()

   103→def resolve_template_dir(skill_root: Path, root: Path, locale_value: str) -> Path:
   104→    templates_root = skill_root / "assets" / "templates"
   105→    locale_name = normalize_locale(locale_value, root)
   106→    template_dir = templates_root / locale_name
   107→    if not template_dir.exists():

   109→    return template_dir

   112→def find_external_policy_sources(root: Path) -> list[str]:
   113→    matches: set[str] = set()
   114→    for pattern in ALWAYS_POLICY_SOURCE_PATTERNS:
   115→        for path in root.glob(pattern):
   116→            if path.is_file():
   117→                matches.add(path.relative_to(root).as_posix())
   118→    for pattern in CONTENT_POLICY_SOURCE_PATTERNS:
   119→        for path in root.glob(pattern):
   120→            if path.is_file() and has_policy_content(path):
   121→                matches.add(path.relative_to(root).as_posix())
   122→    return sorted(matches)

   124→
   125→def has_policy_content(path: Path) -> bool:
   126→    try:
   127→        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore").lower()
   128→    except OSError:
   129→        return True
   130→    return any(re.search(pattern, text) for pattern in POLICY_TEXT_PATTERNS)

   132→
   133→def find_latest_version(folder: Path, pattern: str) -> str | None:
   134→    regex = re.compile(pattern)
   135→    versions = []
   136→    if folder.exists():
   137→        for path in folder.iterdir():
   138→            match = regex.fullmatch(path.name)
   139→            if match:
   140→                versions.append(match.group(1))
   141→    return max(versions) if versions else None
   142→
   143→
   144→def find_latest_goal_file(folder: Path) -> Path | None:
   145→    regex = re.compile(r"goal_v(\d{3})_(\d{8})\.md")
   146→    matches: list[tuple[str, str, Path]] = []
   147→    if folder.exists():
   148→        for path in folder.iterdir():
   149→            match = regex.fullmatch(path.name)
   150→            if match:
   151→                matches.append((match.group(1), match.group(2), path))
   152→    if not matches:
   153→        return None
   154→    matches.sort()
   155→    return matches[-1][2]
   156→
   157→
   158→def find_latest_todo_version(folder: Path, goal_version: str) -> str | None:
   159→    regex = re.compile(rf"todo_v{goal_version}_(\d{{3}})_\d{{8}}\.md")
   160→    versions = []
   161→    if folder.exists():
   162→        for path in folder.iterdir():
   163→            match = regex.fullmatch(path.name)
   164→            if match:
   165→                versions.append(match.group(1))
   166→    return max(versions) if versions else None

   169→def find_latest_todo_file(folder: Path, goal_version: str) -> Path | None:
   170→    regex = re.compile(rf"todo_v{goal_version}_(\d{{3}})_(\d{{8}})\.md")
   171→    matches: list[tuple[str, str, Path]] = []

   174→            match = regex.fullmatch(path.name)

   176→                matches.append((match.group(1), match.group(2), path))

   183→def find_latest_chat_log_file(folder: Path) -> Path | None:
   184→    regex = re.compile(r"(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})_(\d{2}-\d{2})\.md")
   185→    matches: list[tuple[str, str, Path]] = []
   186→    if folder.exists():
   187→        for path in folder.iterdir():
   188→            match = regex.fullmatch(path.name)
   189→            if match:
   190→                matches.append((match.group(1), match.group(2), path))
   191→    if not matches:

   194→    return matches[-1][2]

   197→def write_template(template_path: Path, target_path: Path, force: bool, values: dict[str, str]) -> bool:
   198→    if target_path.exists() and not force:
   199→        return False
   200→    content = template_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   201→    for key, value in values.items():
   202→        content = content.replace(f"{{{{{key}}}}}", value)
   203→    target_path.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
   204→    return True
   205→
   206→
   207→@dataclass
   208→class ScaffoldPaths:
   209→    root: Path
   210→    goal_dir: Path
   211→    todo_dir: Path
   212→    chat_dir: Path
   213→    goal_path: Path
   214→    todo_path: Path
   215→    chat_path: Path
   216→    goal_version: str
   217→    todo_version: str
   218→    previous_version: str
   219→    date_label: str
   220→    time_label: str
   221→
   222→
   223→def resolve_scaffold_paths(root: Path, dt: datetime) -> ScaffoldPaths:
   224→    date_token = dt.strftime("%Y%m%d")
   225→    date_label = dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
   226→    time_label = dt.strftime("%H:%M")
   227→    chat_filename = dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M.md")
   228→
   229→    goal_dir = root / "Memory" / "01_goals"
   230→    todo_dir = root / "Memory" / "02_todos"
   231→    chat_dir = root / "Memory" / "03_chat_logs"
   232→
   233→    goal_version = find_latest_version(goal_dir, r"goal_v(\d{3})_\d{8}\.md")
   234→    if goal_version is None:
   235→        goal_version = "001"
   236→    previous_version = f"v{int(goal_version) - 1:03d}" if int(goal_version) > 1 else "none"
   237→
   238→    todo_version = find_latest_todo_version(todo_dir, goal_version)
   239→    if todo_version is None:
   240→        todo_version = "001"
   241→
   242→    goal_path = find_latest_goal_file(goal_dir) or goal_dir / f"goal_v{goal_version}_{date_token}.md"
   243→    todo_path = (
   244→        find_latest_todo_file(todo_dir, goal_version)
   245→        or todo_dir / f"todo_v{goal_version}_{todo_version}_{date_token}.md"
   246→    )
   247→    chat_path = find_latest_chat_log_file(chat_dir) or chat_dir / chat_filename
   248→
   249→    return ScaffoldPaths(
   250→        root=root,
   251→        goal_dir=goal_dir,
   252→        todo_dir=todo_dir,
   253→        chat_dir=chat_dir,
   254→        goal_path=goal_path,
   255→        todo_path=todo_path,
   256→        chat_path=chat_path,
   257→        goal_version=goal_version,
   258→        todo_version=todo_version,
   259→        previous_version=previous_version,
   260→        date_label=date_label,
   261→        time_label=time_label,
   262→    )
   263→
   264→
   265→def create_memory_dirs(root: Path) -> list[str]:
   266→    created: list[str] = []
   267→    for rel in DIRS:
   268→        path = root / rel
   269→        if not path.exists():
   270→            created.append(path.relative_to(root).as_posix())
   271→        path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   272→    return created
   273→
   274→
   275→def scaffold_content_files(
   276→    paths: ScaffoldPaths,
   277→    templates: Path,
   278→    goal_summary: str,
   279→    project_name: str,
   280→    force: bool,
   281→) -> tuple[list[str], list[str]]:
   282→    created: list[str] = []
   283→    preserved: list[str] = []
   284→    pairs = [
   285→        (
   286→            templates / "goal.md",
   287→            paths.goal_path,
   288→            {
   289→                "GOAL_VERSION": paths.goal_version,
   290→                "DATE": paths.date_label,
   291→                "PREVIOUS_VERSION": paths.previous_version,
   292→                "GOAL_SUMMARY": goal_summary.strip(),
   293→            },
   294→        ),
   295→        (
   296→            templates / "todo.md",
   297→            paths.todo_path,
   298→            {
   299→                "GOAL_VERSION": paths.goal_version,
   300→                "TODO_SUBVERSION": paths.todo_version,
   301→                "DATE": paths.date_label,
   302→            },
   303→        ),
   304→        (
   305→            templates / "chat-log.md",
   306→            paths.chat_path,
   307→            {
   308→                "DATE": paths.date_label,
   309→                "TIME": paths.time_label,
   310→                "PROJECT_NAME": project_name.strip(),
   311→            },
   312→        ),
   313→    ]
   314→    for template_path, target_path, values in pairs:
   315→        if write_template(template_path, target_path, force, values):
   316→            created.append(target_path.relative_to(paths.root).as_posix())
   317→        else:
   318→            preserved.append(target_path.relative_to(paths.root).as_posix())
   319→    return created, preserved

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→recall-session — On-demand transcript sync + latest session lookup.
     4→
     5→Scans all three client transcript directories for the current project,
     6→imports any new transcripts, queries the global index to find the most
     7→recent previous session, and outputs its metadata and clean transcript
     8→path so the model can generate a continuation summary.
     9→
    10→Usage:
    11→  uv run python recall-session.py --root /path/to/repo
    12→  uv run python recall-session.py --root . --activation-time 2026-03-15T10:00:00Z
    13→"""
    14→
    15→from __future__ import annotations
    16→
    17→import argparse
    18→import json
    19→import sqlite3
    20→import sys
    21→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    22→from pathlib import Path
    23→
    24→from _transcript_discover import (
    25→    default_global_transcript_root,
    26→    discover_source_files,
    27→    infer_project_slug,
    28→    transcript_matches_repo,
    29→)
    30→from _transcript_import import import_transcript, transcript_has_content
    31→from _transcript_parse import parse_transcript, slugify
    32→
    33→
    34→def main() -> int:
    35→    args = parse_args()
    36→    root = Path(args.root).resolve()
    37→    if not root.exists():
    38→        raise SystemExit(f"root does not exist: {root}")
    39→
    40→    repo_slug = slugify(args.project_name.strip() or root.name, fallback="project")
    41→    global_root = resolve_global_root(args.global_root)
    42→    activation_time = args.activation_time or _now_iso()
    43→
    44→    # Step 1: On-demand sync — discover and import for current project only
    45→    imported = _sync_current_project(root, repo_slug, global_root)
    46→
    47→    # Step 2: Query global index for latest session
    48→    session = _find_latest_session(global_root, root, repo_slug, activation_time)
    49→
    50→    if session is None:
    51→        payload = {
    52→            "found": False,
    53→            "project": repo_slug,
    54→            "repo": root.as_posix(),
    55→            "imported_count": imported,
    56→            "message": "No previous session found for this project.",
    57→        }
    58→    else:
    59→        clean_path = session.get("global_clean_path", "")
    60→        clean_content = ""
    61→        if clean_path and Path(clean_path).exists():
    62→            try:
    63→                clean_content = Path(clean_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    64→            except OSError:
    65→                pass
    66→
    67→        payload = {
    68→            "found": True,
    69→            "project": repo_slug,
    70→            "repo": root.as_posix(),
    71→            "imported_count": imported,
    72→            "client": session.get("client", ""),
    73→            "session_id": session.get("session_id", ""),
    74→            "title": session.get("title", ""),
    75→            "started_at": session.get("started_at", ""),
    76→            "cwd": session.get("cwd", ""),
    77→            "branch": session.get("branch", ""),
    78→            "message_count": session.get("message_count", 0),
    79→            "tool_event_count": session.get("tool_event_count", 0),
    80→            "global_clean_path": clean_path,
    81→            "clean_content": clean_content,
    82→        }
    83→
    84→    if args.format == "json":
    85→        print(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False))
    86→    else:
    87→        print(format_text(payload))
    88→    return 0
    89→
    90→
    91→def _sync_current_project(root: Path, repo_slug: str, global_root: Path) -> int:
    92→    """Discover and import transcripts for the current project only."""
    93→    discovered = discover_source_files()
    94→    imported = 0
    95→
    96→    for client, source in discovered:
    97→        try:
    98→            parsed = parse_transcript(client, source)
    99→        except Exception:
   100→            continue
   101→
   102→        if not transcript_has_content(parsed):
   103→            continue
   104→
   105→        if not transcript_matches_repo(parsed, root, repo_slug):
   106→            continue
   107→
   108→        try:
   109→            import_transcript(
   110→                parsed=parsed,
   111→                root=root,
   112→                global_root=global_root,
   113→                project_slug=repo_slug,
   114→                raw_upload_permission="not-set",
   115→                mirror_to_repo=True,
   116→            )
   117→            imported += 1
   118→        except Exception:
   119→            continue
   120→
   121→    return imported
   122→
   123→
   124→def _find_latest_session(
   125→    global_root: Path,
   126→    root: Path,
   127→    repo_slug: str,
   128→    activation_time: str,
   129→) -> dict | None:
   130→    """Query the SQLite index for the latest session before activation_time."""
   131→    db_path = global_root / "index" / "search.sqlite"
   132→    if not db_path.exists():
   133→        # Fallback: try sessions.jsonl
   134→        return _find_latest_from_jsonl(global_root, root, repo_slug, activation_time)
   135→
   136→    try:
   137→        conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
   138→        try:
   139→            conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
   140→            cursor = conn.execute(
   141→                """
   142→                SELECT * FROM transcripts
   143→                WHERE started_at < ?
   144→                ORDER BY started_at DESC
   145→                LIMIT 20
   146→                """,
   147→                (activation_time,),
   148→            )
   149→            rows = cursor.fetchall()
   150→        finally:
   151→            conn.close()
   152→    except (sqlite3.Error, OSError):
   153→        return _find_latest_from_jsonl(global_root, root, repo_slug, activation_time)
   154→
   155→    resolved_root = root.resolve().as_posix()
   156→    for row in rows:
   157→        row_dict = dict(row)
   158→        cwd = row_dict.get("cwd", "")
   159→        project = row_dict.get("project", "")
   160→        # Match by cwd path or project slug
   161→        if _cwd_matches(cwd, resolved_root) or project == repo_slug:
   162→            return row_dict
   163→
   164→    return None
   165→
   166→
   167→def _find_latest_from_jsonl(
   168→    global_root: Path,
   169→    root: Path,
   170→    repo_slug: str,
   171→    activation_time: str,
   172→) -> dict | None:
   173→    """Fallback: scan sessions.jsonl when SQLite index doesn't exist."""
   174→    jsonl_path = global_root / "index" / "sessions.jsonl"
   175→    if not jsonl_path.exists():
   176→        return None
   177→
   178→    resolved_root = root.resolve().as_posix()
   179→    candidates: list[dict] = []
   180→
   181→    try:
   182→        with jsonl_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
   183→            for line in fh:
   184→                line = line.strip()
   185→                if not line:
   186→                    continue
   187→                try:
   188→                    entry = json.loads(line)
   189→                except json.JSONDecodeError:
   190→                    continue
   191→
   192→                started = entry.get("started_at", "")
   193→                if started >= activation_time:
   194→                    continue
   195→
   196→                cwd = entry.get("cwd", "")
   197→                project = entry.get("project", "")
   198→                if _cwd_matches(cwd, resolved_root) or project == repo_slug:
   199→                    candidates.append(entry)
   200→    except OSError:
   201→        return None
   202→
   203→    if not candidates:
   204→        return None
   205→
   206→    # Sort by started_at descending, return latest
   207→    candidates.sort(key=lambda e: e.get("started_at", ""), reverse=True)
   208→    return candidates[0]
   209→
   210→
   211→def _cwd_matches(cwd: str, resolved_root: str) -> bool:
   212→    """Check if a transcript's cwd matches the repo root (exact or subdirectory)."""
   213→    if not cwd:
   214→        return False
   215→    try:
   216→        cwd_resolved = Path(cwd).resolve().as_posix()
   217→        if cwd_resolved == resolved_root:
   218→            return True
   219→        # Check if cwd is a subdirectory of resolved_root
   220→        return cwd_resolved.startswith(resolved_root + "/")
   221→    except OSError:
   222→        return False
   223→
   224→
   225→def _now_iso() -> str:
   226→    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
   227→
   228→
   229→def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
   230→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   231→        description="On-demand transcript sync and latest session recall for MemoryTree.",
   232→    )
   233→    parser.add_argument("--root", default=".", help="Target repository root.")
   234→    parser.add_argument(
   235→        "--project-name",
   236→        default="",
   237→        help="Project label. Defaults to the repo folder name.",
   238→    )
   239→    parser.add_argument(
   240→        "--global-root",
   241→        default="",
   242→        help="Override the global transcript root. Defaults to ~/.memorytree/transcripts.",
   243→    )
   244→    parser.add_argument(
   245→        "--activation-time",
   246→        default="",
   247→        help="ISO timestamp of session activation. Transcripts started at or after this time are excluded.",
   248→    )
   249→    parser.add_argument(
   250→        "--format",
   251→        choices=("text", "json"),
   252→        default="text",
   253→        help="Output format.",
   254→    )
   255→    return parser.parse_args()
   256→
   257→
   258→def resolve_global_root(path_value: str) -> Path:
   259→    if path_value:
   260→        return Path(path_value).expanduser().resolve()
   261→    return default_global_transcript_root()
   262→
   263→
   264→def format_text(payload: dict) -> str:
   265→    if not payload.get("found"):
   266→        return (
   267→            f"project: {payload.get('project', '?')}\n"
   268→            f"imported: {payload.get('imported_count', 0)}\n"
   269→            f"result: {payload.get('message', 'No previous session found.')}"
   270→        )
   271→
   272→    lines = [
   273→        f"project: {payload.get('project', '')}",
   274→        f"client: {payload.get('client', '')}",
   275→        f"session_id: {payload.get('session_id', '')}",
   276→        f"title: {payload.get('title', '')}",
   277→        f"started_at: {payload.get('started_at', '')}",
   278→        f"cwd: {payload.get('cwd', '')}",
   279→        f"branch: {payload.get('branch', '')}",
   280→        f"messages: {payload.get('message_count', 0)}",
   281→        f"tool_events: {payload.get('tool_event_count', 0)}",
   282→        f"imported_this_sync: {payload.get('imported_count', 0)}",
   283→        f"clean_transcript: {payload.get('global_clean_path', '')}",
   284→    ]
   285→
   286→    content = payload.get("clean_content", "")
   287→    if content:
   288→        lines.append("")
   289→        lines.append("--- clean transcript content ---")
   290→        lines.append(content)
   291→
   292→    return "\n".join(lines)
   293→
   294→
   295→if __name__ == "__main__":
   296→    sys.exit(main())
   297→

1→
2→ 3→ 4→ 5→# MemoryTree Workflow 6→ 7→**Persistent, Git-tracked project memory for AI coding assistants.** 8→ 9→[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE) 10→[![Python 3.11+](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-3.11%2B-3776AB.svg?logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://www.python.org/) 11→[![Claude Code](https://img.shields.io/badge/Claude_Code-Skill-cc785c.svg?logo=anthropic&logoColor=white)](https://claude.ai/claude-code) 12→[![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/Platform-macOS%20%7C%20Linux%20%7C%20Windows-lightgrey.svg)](#requirements) 13→ 14→

15→ Install  •  16→ Usage  •  17→ Features  •  18→ Heartbeat  •  19→ Scripts  •  20→ Docs 21→

22→ 23→
24→ 25→--- 26→ 27→## What It Does 28→ 29→MemoryTree gives AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI) a structured memory layer that survives across sessions: 30→ 31→| | Feature | Description | 32→|---|---------|-------------| 33→| :dart: | **Goals** | Track your project's north star across sessions | 34→| :white_check_mark: | **Todos** | Version-bound task lists that stay in sync with goals | 35→| :speech_balloon: | **Chat Logs** | Append-only session records for full traceability | 36→| :books: | **Knowledge** | Durable notes, specs, and architecture decisions (also stored in goals when scope-relevant) | 37→| :inbox_tray: | **Transcripts** | Import and archive AI chat history from Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI | 38→ 39→All stored as plain Markdown files under `Memory/`, tracked by Git, minimal dependencies. 40→ 41→## Install 42→ 43→```bash 44→git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow 45→``` 46→ 47→On Windows (Git Bash): 48→```bash 49→git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow "$USERPROFILE/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow" 50→``` 51→ 52→## Usage 53→ 54→In any Claude Code session, run: 55→ 56→``` 57→/memorytree-workflow 58→``` 59→ 60→The skill will immediately: 61→1. **Detect** — check if the current repo has MemoryTree installed 62→2. **Alert** — display pending notifications from `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` 63→3. **Init** — if missing, scaffold `AGENTS.md` and `Memory/` folders 64→4. **Upgrade** — if partial, add missing pieces without overwriting existing policy 65→5. **Maintain** — if installed, read the active goal/todo and report current state 66→ 67→No manual confirmation needed — the skill acts on the detection result immediately. 68→ 69→## Architecture 70→ 71→### Per-Repository Structure 72→ 73→After initialization, your repo will have: 74→ 75→``` 76→AGENTS.md # AI behavior rules for this repo 77→Memory/ 78→ 01_goals/ # Versioned project goals 79→ 02_todos/ # Task lists bound to goals 80→ 03_chat_logs/ # Append-only session logs 81→ 04_knowledge/ # (optional) Durable project notes 82→ 05_archive/ # (optional) Retired goals/todos 83→ 06_transcripts/ # (optional) AI chat history archive 84→ clean/ # Cleaned Markdown indexes 85→ manifests/ # Import metadata 86→ raw/ # Raw transcript mirrors 87→``` 88→ 89→### Global Directory (`~/.memorytree/`) 90→ 91→Cross-project shared state: 92→ 93→``` 94→~/.memorytree/ 95→ config.toml # User-level settings 96→ alerts.json # Pending notifications 97→ heartbeat.lock # Single-instance lock 98→ logs/ # Heartbeat execution logs 99→ transcripts/ # Global transcript archive 100→ raw/ # By client/project/year/month 101→ clean/ # Cleaned Markdown 102→ index/ 103→ sessions.jsonl # Session metadata 104→ search.sqlite # Full-text search index 105→``` 106→ 107→For the full schema see [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md). 108→ 109→## Features 110→ 111→### Memory Management 112→ 113→| Memory Type | Behavior | 114→|-------------|----------| 115→| **Goals** | Changed only after explicit user confirmation of a scope or requirement change. Versioned. | 116→| **Todos** | Kept in sync with the active goal. Updated when progress changes or next task becomes clearer. | 117→| **Chat Logs** | Append-only. Never rewritten or deleted. | 118→| **Knowledge** | Stores architecture decisions, product ideas, operating constraints. Goals may also hold scope-relevant items. | 119→| **Archive** | Stale context moved here or to knowledge files to keep active files concise. | 120→ 121→### Multi-Client Transcript Import 122→ 123→Import AI chat history from three clients: 124→ 125→| Client | Source | 126→|--------|--------| 127→| **Codex** | `~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl` | 128→| **Claude Code** | `transcript_path` if exposed; otherwise `~/.claude/projects//*.jsonl` | 129→| **Gemini CLI** | Saved chats first; checkpoints under `~/.gemini/tmp//checkpoints` if enabled | 130→ 131→Key principles: 132→ 133→- Raw transcripts preserved unchanged as source of truth 134→- Cleaned Markdown generated by **deterministic code** (not model tokens) 135→- Current repo only stores transcripts belonging to this project; others go to global archive only 136→- First import asks once whether raw transcripts may be committed to the repo 137→ 138→### Cross-Project Search 139→ 140→When searching all memories or chat history: 141→ 142→1. Query `search.sqlite` for fast full-text lookup 143→2. Use `sessions.jsonl` for session-level metadata filtering (client, project, date range) 144→3. Load matching cleaned Markdown for context; confirm exact wording against raw transcripts 145→4. Combine with the current repo's `Memory/` files 146→5. Fall back to scanning `clean/` directory if the index does not exist 147→ 148→### Session Continuity 149→ 150→When you open a new session and want to continue where you left off, ask MemoryTree to show your most recent conversation: 151→ 152→> "Look at my last chat" / "看看我最近的聊天" / "Continue where I left off" 153→ 154→The skill will: 155→ 156→1. **Instant sync** — immediately scan all three client directories (not waiting for the scheduled heartbeat) 157→2. **Locate** — find the latest session for the current project across Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI 158→3. **Summarize** — generate a continuation summary: what was discussed, what was tried, and what remains unresolved 159→4. **Handoff** — you can say "continue" and pick up exactly where the previous session stopped 160→ 161→This works **cross-client**: a Codex session can be recovered from Claude Code, and vice versa. 162→ 163→For details see [`references/transcript-archive.md`](references/transcript-archive.md) and [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md). 164→ 165→### Git Safety 166→ 167→MemoryTree follows strict isolation rules to never interfere with your repo's workflow: 168→ 169→| Rule | Description | 170→|------|-------------| 171→| File isolation | Only stages/commits `Memory/**` (excluding `raw/` until approved) and `AGENTS.md` (when managed by MemoryTree) | 172→| Branch isolation | Uses dedicated branch `memorytree/--` | 173→| Commit format | `memorytree(): ` or repo-compatible equivalent | 174→| PR isolation | Separate PR for MemoryTree-only changes | 175→| Auto-merge | Only when repo still enforces its review and CI checks | 176→| Auto-push | On by default; skips if no remote; fast-forward only | 177→| Protected branches | Never overrides branch protection or CI rules | 178→ 179→The skill stops and asks before proceeding in 8 scenarios: mixed product code, AGENTS.md conflicts, unclear target branch, repo forbids bot pushes, CI conflicts, unknown raw transcript permission, cross-project transcripts, or protected branch refusal. 180→ 181→For the full policy see [`references/git-policy.md`](references/git-policy.md). 182→ 183→### Sensitive Info Scanning 184→ 185→During transcript cleaning, the heartbeat scans for API keys, passwords, tokens, and other secrets: 186→ 187→- Matches are logged as warnings and written to `alerts.json` 188→- **No automatic deletion or redaction** — the user decides how to handle them 189→ 190→### Locale Support 191→ 192→| Feature | Description | 193→|---------|-------------| 194→| Templates | English (`en`) and Simplified Chinese (`zh-cn`) | 195→| Auto-detect | Detects language from repo content first, then system locale | 196→| Reply language | Follows user explicit request > message language > repo locale > template locale > `en` | 197→| Non-destructive | Never rewrites existing files just to translate them | 198→ 199→### Environment Adaptation 200→ 201→MemoryTree does not assume the target repo has a Python environment: 202→ 203→``` 204→Prefer uv run python → fall back to system python → fall back to direct template scaffolding 205→``` 206→ 207→Never auto-creates `.venv` or `pyproject.toml` inside the project. 208→ 209→## Background Heartbeat 210→ 211→The heartbeat automates transcript discovery, import, cleaning, commit, and push without consuming model tokens or requiring human intervention. 212→ 213→**Execution flow**: 214→ 215→``` 216→Acquire lock → Load config.toml → Iterate projects → Discover → Import 217→→ Clean → Commit → (auto_push) Push → Release lock → Exit 218→``` 219→ 220→### Install the daemon 221→ 222→```bash 223→memorytree-daemon install 224→``` 225→ 226→### CLI commands 227→ 228→| Command | Description | 229→|-----------------------------|-------------| 230→| `memorytree-daemon install` | Register the heartbeat with the OS scheduler (cron / launchd / Task Scheduler). | 231→| `memorytree-daemon uninstall` | Remove the scheduled heartbeat task. | 232→| `memorytree-daemon run-once` | Run a single heartbeat cycle immediately. | 233→| `memorytree-daemon watch` | Continuous loop for development and debugging only. | 234→| `memorytree-daemon status` | Show registration state and last execution result. | 235→ 236→### Key settings (`~/.memorytree/config.toml`) 237→ 238→| Setting | Default | Description | 239→|----------------------|----------|-------------| 240→| `auto_push` | `true` | Push to remote after each commit. Skips if no remote is configured. | 241→| `heartbeat_interval` | `"5m"` | Interval between heartbeat executions. | 242→| `log_level` | `"info"` | Log verbosity (`debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error`). | 243→ 244→### Design constraints 245→ 246→- Single execution, stateless, idempotent — safe to re-run at any interval 247→- One computer = one scheduled heartbeat task 248→- Will not silently register — always confirms before `install` 249→ 250→For details see [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) and [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md). 251→ 252→## Design Principles 253→ 254→| Principle | How | 255→|-----------|-----| 256→| **Zero intrusion** | Never changes the repo's branch model, CI, or review process | 257→| **Minimal dependencies** | Scripts target Python standard library; no third-party packages required at install time | 258→| **Deterministic** | Transcript cleaning done by code, not model tokens | 259→| **Single source of truth** | Each concept defined in one reference file, others cross-reference | 260→| **Spec-driven** | Reference docs define the contract; code implements it faithfully | 261→| **User asset** | Memory belongs to the user; auto_push on by default to prevent local data loss | 262→ 263→## Scripts 264→ 265→All scripts live under `scripts/` and target Python 3.11+ stdlib only (no third-party packages). 266→ 267→### Entry Points 268→ 269→| Script | Purpose | 270→|--------|---------| 271→| [`heartbeat.py`](scripts/heartbeat.py) | Background heartbeat — discover, import, clean, commit, push transcripts | 272→| [`memorytree_daemon.py`](scripts/memorytree_daemon.py) | Daemon lifecycle manager: `install` / `uninstall` / `run-once` / `watch` / `status` | 273→| [`recall-session.py`](scripts/recall-session.py) | On-demand transcript sync and latest session recall for cross-session context recovery | 274→| [`init-memorytree.py`](scripts/init-memorytree.py) | Scaffold `Memory/` and `AGENTS.md` for a new repository | 275→| [`upgrade-memorytree.py`](scripts/upgrade-memorytree.py) | Add missing MemoryTree pieces to a partial repository | 276→| [`import-transcripts.py`](scripts/import-transcripts.py) | Import one transcript into repo mirror and global archive | 277→| [`discover-transcripts.py`](scripts/discover-transcripts.py) | Scan local client stores and mirror matching transcripts | 278→| [`detect-memorytree-locale.py`](scripts/detect-memorytree-locale.py) | Print the effective locale for a target repository | 279→ 280→### Internal Modules 281→ 282→| Module | Purpose | 283→|--------|---------| 284→| [`_transcript_utils.py`](scripts/_transcript_utils.py) | Transcript parsing (Codex/Claude/Gemini), import, SQLite indexing, discovery | 285→| [`_config_utils.py`](scripts/_config_utils.py) | Load/validate `~/.memorytree/config.toml` with `tomllib` | 286→| [`_alert_utils.py`](scripts/_alert_utils.py) | Manage `alerts.json` — dedup, threshold-based alerting, failure counts | 287→| [`_lock_utils.py`](scripts/_lock_utils.py) | PID-based lock file with atomic creation and stale lock detection | 288→| [`_log_utils.py`](scripts/_log_utils.py) | Date-rotated file logging to `~/.memorytree/logs/` | 289→| [`_scaffold_utils.py`](scripts/_scaffold_utils.py) | Template rendering and directory scaffolding helpers | 290→| [`_locale_utils.py`](scripts/_locale_utils.py) | Locale detection and normalization | 291→ 292→## Reference Docs 293→ 294→| File | Purpose | 295→|------|---------| 296→| [`references/project-detection.md`](references/project-detection.md) | Detect install state | 297→| [`references/memory-layout.md`](references/memory-layout.md) | Folder and file naming rules | 298→| [`references/update-rules.md`](references/update-rules.md) | When to version goals, todos, and session logs | 299→| [`references/git-policy.md`](references/git-policy.md) | Git defaults, auto-push, and policy merge | 300→| [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) | Heartbeat architecture, daemon CLI, execution flow | 301→| [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md) | `~/.memorytree/` layout, `config.toml` schema, `alerts.json` format | 302→| [`references/transcript-archive.md`](references/transcript-archive.md) | Transcript archival, cleaning, search, and upload rules | 303→| [`references/locale-selection.md`](references/locale-selection.md) | Locale choice rules | 304→| [`references/response-language.md`](references/response-language.md) | Reply-language precedence | 305→| [`references/execution-environment.md`](references/execution-environment.md) | How to run scripts without a project-local Python environment | 306→| [`references/upgrade-path.md`](references/upgrade-path.md) | Safe adoption flow for partial repos | 307→ 308→## Update 309→ 310→```bash 311→cd ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow && git pull 312→``` 313→ 314→## Requirements 315→ 316→- [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code) CLI 317→- Python 3.11+ (for transcript and heartbeat scripts; `tomllib` is stdlib in 3.11+) 318→- Git 319→ 320→## License 321→ 322→MIT 323→

1→"""Transcript search index — SQLite upsert for the global transcript catalog."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import sqlite3
     6→from pathlib import Path
     7→from typing import Any
     8→
     9→
    10→def upsert_search_index(db_path: Path, manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    11→    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
    12→    try:
    13→        conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
    14→        conn.execute(
    15→            """
    16→            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts (
    17→                client TEXT NOT NULL,
    18→                project TEXT NOT NULL,
    19→                session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    20→                raw_sha256 TEXT NOT NULL,
    21→                title TEXT NOT NULL,
    22→                started_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    23→                imported_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    24→                cwd TEXT NOT NULL,
    25→                branch TEXT NOT NULL,
    26→                raw_source_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    27→                raw_upload_permission TEXT NOT NULL,
    28→                global_raw_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    29→                global_clean_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    30→                repo_raw_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    31→                repo_clean_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    32→                repo_manifest_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    33→                message_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
    34→                tool_event_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
    35→                PRIMARY KEY (client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)
    36→            )
    37→            """
    38→        )
    39→        conn.execute(
    40→            """
    41→            INSERT INTO transcripts (
    42→                client,
    43→                project,
    44→                session_id,
    45→                raw_sha256,
    46→                title,
    47→                started_at,
    48→                imported_at,
    49→                cwd,
    50→                branch,
    51→                raw_source_path,
    52→                raw_upload_permission,
    53→                global_raw_path,
    54→                global_clean_path,
    55→                repo_raw_path,
    56→                repo_clean_path,
    57→                repo_manifest_path,
    58→                message_count,
    59→                tool_event_count
    60→            ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
    61→            ON CONFLICT(client, project, session_id, raw_sha256) DO UPDATE SET
    62→                title = excluded.title,
    63→                started_at = excluded.started_at,
    64→                imported_at = excluded.imported_at,
    65→                cwd = excluded.cwd,
    66→                branch = excluded.branch,
